remaining_tasks.name: Update STATE.md with decisions + next routing

blockers.description: VERIFY-03 SC-3 FAIL: combined posterior MAP=0.86 (expected 0.73). Hypotheses: (A) D(h) missing from --combine code path, (B) coordinate mismatch between pre-fix CRBs (equatorial qS/phiS) and post-fix galaxy catalog (ecliptic). Fix phase required before Phase 41/42.

decisions.decision: Q1 VERIFY-03 MAP=0.86: Insert fix phase (Option B). Audit angle mapping independently, then check D(h) in --combine.
decisions.rationale: Cannot declare Phase 40 PASS with unknown MAP bias. User wants angle-mapping verified first, then D(h) hypothesis tested.

decisions.decision: Q2 VERIFY-05 borderline Phase 41: Skip. 19 off-grid events noted as known limitation.
decisions.rationale: 0.041 below 0.05 threshold; accept for now with documented consequence.

decisions.decision: Q3 VERIFY-04 Phase 42: Defer until Q1 resolved. Anisotropy (STAGE-2-TRIGGER) may be symptom of same coordinate/D(h) bug.
decisions.rationale: User decision — anisotropy result unreliable if MAP is biased.

next_action: Resume 40-06: spawn fresh gsd-executor with the three user decisions (Q1=B, Q2=skip, Q3=defer) and remaining tasks 3-5. Then plan the fix phase (angle audit + D(h) diagnosis).
context_notes: The MAP=0.86 surprise likely stems from a coordinate mismatch created by the COORD fix: CRBs on disk store equatorial (qS,phiS) from pre-fix simulation, but the v2.2 galaxy catalog now stores ecliptic coordinates. This breaks the sim/eval self-consistency that v2.1 had (both sides were wrong, but consistently so). The fix phase must audit this specifically.
